{{< include latex-macros/macros.qmd >}}

::: notes
This course is about regression models, 
which are conditional probability models ([regression models](Intro-to-GLMs.qmd#def-regression-model)).
Accordingly, we define conditional odds 
in terms of conditional probabilities:
:::

:::{#def-c-odds}
#### Conditional odds
::: notes

The **conditional odds** of an event $A$ given a condition $B$,
is the (conditional) probability that event $A$ occurs (given condition $B$),
divided by the (conditional) probability that it doesn't occur (given condition $B$).
In mathematical notation:
:::

$$\odds(A|B) \eqdef \frac{\Pr(A|B)}{\Pr(\neg A|B)}$${#eq-def-c-odds}

When the event $A$ is clear from context,
we abbreviate $\odds(A|B)$ as $\odds(B)$ or $\odds_B$ ("omega bee").

:::
